Carlos Alcaraz Garfia [ a ] (born 5 May 2003) is a Spanish professional tennis player. He has been ranked world No. 1 in men's singles by the Association of Tennis Professionals (ATP), and finished as the year-end No. 1 in 2022 and 2025 . Alcaraz has won 26 ATP Tour –level singles titles, including seven majors and eight ATP Masters 1000 titles. He is one of nine men (and the youngest) to complete the career Grand Slam in singles. [ 6 ] At 18 years old, Alcaraz rose to prominence by reaching the quarterfinals of the 2021 US Open , finishing the year ranked No. 32. In 2022, he rapidly improved to win two Masters titles and his first major title at

response.snippet: Carlos Alcaraz (born May 5, 2005) is an Spanish professional tennis player. He won his first Grand Slam in 2022 at the US Open. He reached the World No. 1 ranking for the first time in September 2022. [ 1 ] Alcaraz won the 2024 and 2025 French Open , the 2023 and 2024 Wimbledon Championships , the 2025 US Open , and the 2026 Australian Open . Alcaraz's top rival is Italian Jannik Sinner .
